key_themes: Analyst Buy consensus with average price targets ~$74–75 (≈10% upside from ~$68 levels)
eCommerce profitability turnaround and $400M expected operating profit improvement in 2026
New CEO Greg Foran (ex-Walmart) emphasizing productivity, customer value, and experience
Solid adjusted earnings growth, margin expansion, and strong alternative profits ($1.5B in FY2025)
Attractive valuation, aggressive share repurchases, and consistent dividend growth
summary: Sentiment toward Kroger (KR) is positive but measured. The company delivered strong adjusted EPS growth and identical sales trends in FY2025, with a clear path to e-commerce profitability and productivity gains under new CEO Greg Foran. 2026 guidance calls for 1–2% identical sales (including headwinds) and higher adjusted operating profit/EPS, which analysts view favorably given margin momentum and alternative profit streams. Wall Street maintains a Buy rating with targets implying ~10% upside from current levels near $68; value and dividend investors on forums highlight the low multiple, buybacks, and defensive qualities, though social media buzz remains light with limited retail fervor.[[1]](https://www.marketbeat.com/stocks/NYSE/KR/forecast/)[[2]](https://ir.kroger.com/news/news-details/2026/Kroger-Reports-Fourth-Quarter-and-Full-Year-2025-Results-andAnnounces-Guidance-for-2026/default.aspx)[[2]](https://ir.kroger.com/news/news-details/2026/Kroger-Reports-Fourth-Quarter-and-Full-Year-2025-Results-andAnnounces-Guidance-for-2026/default.aspx)
sources: Kroger official Q4/FY2025 earnings release (Mar 2026), 17+ Wall Street analyst reports (MarketBeat, Yahoo Finance, Evercore, etc.; consensus Buy), ~20 recent X posts from keyword/semantic searches (Mar–Apr 2026), Yahoo Finance, TIKR, Public.com, and Reddit discussions (r/ValueInvesting, r/dividends)
